content.content:
title:	DATA-4715: Archive audit logs to S3 on successful erasure
state:	open
author:	STyagi7
labels:	
assignees:	
reviewers:	LyndonHB
comments:	6
number:	62
url:	https://gitlab.com/HnBI/dns/dns-de-rte-orchestrator/-/merge_requests/62
--
## What

Implements audit log archival to S3 when an erasure request is successfully processed. Once a request reaches the `done` state, the process Lambda materialises all DynamoDB audit log entries for that id to S3 as a gzipped JSON Lines object (`{prefix}/{id}.jsonl.gz`), then deletes the rows from DynamoDB. The existing `FallbackAuditLogReader` (used by the reporter Lambda) seamlessly serves reads from S3 after archival with no changes needed on the read path.

Also fixes two gaps surfaced during code review: a missing 409 response for duplicate erasure request IDs, and `BatchWriteItem` throttle failures silently dropping deletes.

Key files: `audit_logging.py`, `request_processor.py`, `processor_lambda.py`, `creator_lambda.py`, `template.yaml`, `README.md`.

## Why

DATA-4715 — audit log entries were accumulating in DynamoDB indefinitely with no archival path. The S3 fallback reader already existed but had no writer. This ticket closes that gap and reduces long-term DynamoDB table growth.

## Changes

**Audit log archival (core feature)**
- Added `S3AuditLogWriter` — symmetric counterpart to the existing `S3AuditLogReader`, writes gzipped JSONL to the same key format (`{prefix}/{id}.jsonl.gz`)
- Added `DynamoDBAuditLogger.delete_logs()` — batch-deletes all entries for an id using `BatchWriteItem` in chunks of 25, with capped exponential backoff retry on `UnprocessedItems` (up to 5 attempts) to prevent silent partial deletes under throttling
- Added `AuditArchiver` — orchestrates S3-write-then-DynamoDB-delete with safe ordering: if S3 write fails, DynamoDB rows are preserved and reads remain correct
- Added `get_audit_log_archiver()` factory wired from env vars
- `RequestProcessor` accepts an optional `AuditArchiver`; triggers archival after `process_request` completes when `request.state == "done"` and not in dry-run mode; archival failures are caught and logged without re-raising (request is already `done`)
- `processor_lambda.py` constructs the archiver at startup, gracefully degrades to `None` if env vars are absent
- `template.yaml`: added `ProcessS3WritePolicy` (`s3:PutObject` on the audit bucket/prefix) and `AUDIT_LOG_S3_BUCKET`/`AUDIT_LOG_S3_PREFIX` env vars to `ProcessFunction`, mirroring the existing `ReporterS3ReadPolicy` pattern

**Bug fixes**
- `creator_lambda.py`: `ConditionalCheckFailedException` from DynamoDB now returns HTTP 409 Conflict instead of 500, so callers can distinguish duplicate IDs from infrastructure errors
- `request_processor.py`: fixed double-assignment bug in `_transition_request` (`message = message = " due to: " + reason` → `message = message + " due to: " + reason`) that was corrupting audit messages when a `reason` was supplied
- `request_processor.py`: added `case "done": pass` so the match is not a dead-end when a phase transitions to `done` within the same `process_request` call

**Tests (new file + extensions)**
- New `tests/test_s3_audit_log_writer.py` — moto-backed round-trip tests confirming writer and reader are format-compatible
- Extended `tests/test_audit_logging.py` — unit tests for `S3AuditLogWriter`, `AuditArchiver` ordering (S3 before Dynamo; no delete if S3 raises; no-op on empty), `delete_logs` chunking and `UnprocessedItems` retry
- Extended `tests/test_dynamodb_audit_logger.py` — moto integration tests for `delete_logs` and a test confirming DynamoDB entries survive when S3 is unavailable
- Extended `tests/test_request_processor.py` — archiver triggered on `done`, not triggered in dry-run, exception does not propagate
- Extended `tests/test_creator_lambda.py` — 409 on duplicate id, 500 on other `ClientError` codes

**Housekeeping**
- `README.md`: documented `POST /erasure-request` (including 409), audit log lifecycle, and all five process Lambda env vars
- `TODO.md`: ticked off audit log S3 materialisation; added `need-more-detail` handling as an open item

--

content.text:
Here are all 6 unresolved comments on MR !62:

---

**`audit_logging.py` line 201** — Retry isolation + sleep explanation
> "I think this retry stuff would be best isolated a little more into its own code. I am also unsure at a glance what the `time.sleep` call does. Why are we doing a left bitwise shift?"

The `1 << attempt` is exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s, 8s…). Reviewer wants the retry logic extracted into its own function/helper and the bitshift either explained or replaced with `2 ** attempt`.

---

**`request_processor.py` line 189** — Empty `case "done": pass`
> "What's the point in this empty case?"

The reviewer is questioning why there's a no-op match arm.

---

**`request_processor.py` line 201** — Archival call placement
> "Should this not be called by the `case "done"` branch?"

Reviewer suggests the archiver trigger belongs inside the `case "done":` branch rather than after the match block.

---

**`audit_logging.py` line 312** — Sort by timestamp
> "Perhaps sort by timestamp so this is consistent."

The audit log entries returned (likely from `FallbackAuditLogReader` or similar) should be sorted by timestamp for predictable ordering.

---

**`creator_lambda.py` line 157** — Self-note: raise a typed exception from `request_repo`
> "We should take a note and make `request_repo` throw a more sensible exception so it is easier to know what has happened (i.e. a duplicate key was raised)"

A TODO to have `request_repository.py` raise a domain-level exception (e.g. `DuplicateRequestError`) instead of letting the raw `ConditionalCheckFailedException` bubble up to the lambda handler.

---

**`README.md` line 111** — Reorder content
> "Might want to move this around in the README."

The section at line 111 is in the wrong place in the README structure.

---

**Summary:** 6 unresolved comments, all from `LyndonHB`. The two most substantive ones are the retry refactor in `audit_logging.py` and the archiver call placement in `request_processor.py`. Would you like me to address any of these?

31	    """A single audit log entry for an erasure request."""
32	
33	    ID_FIELD: ClassVar[Literal["id"]] = "id"
34	    TIMESTAMP_FIELD: ClassVar[Literal["timestamp"]] = "timestamp"

45	            self.MESSAGE_FIELD: self.message,
46	        }
47	
48	    @classmethod

52	            timestamp=datetime.fromisoformat(data[cls.TIMESTAMP_FIELD]),

54	        )
55

57	class AuditLogger(ABC):
58	    """Base interface for audit logging"""
59	
60	    @abstractmethod
61	    def log(self, id: str, message: str, timestamp: datetime | None = None) -> None:
62	        """
63	        Log an audit log message.
64	
65	        The id should be the same as an erasure requests id
66	
67	        The message can be any free text. You should avoid storing raw personal
68	        information in messages, and make use of anonymisation functions.
69	
70	        The timestamp is optional and if absent will be set to now using the
71	        UTC timezone. All datetimes given will be coereced to UTC.
72	        """
73	        raise NotImplementedError()
74	
75	    @abstractmethod
76	    def get_logs(self, id: str) -> list[AuditEntry]:
77	        """
78	        Retrieve all audit log entries for the given erasure id.
79	
80	        Entries are returned in chronological order (oldest first). Returns
81	        an empty list if no entries exist for the id.
82	        """

87	    """Audit logger implementation that does nothing"""
88

91	        logger.warning("Audit message not saved anywhere: %s", message)
92	        pass

96	        return []

99	class InMemoryAuditLogger(AuditLogger):
100	    """Example implementation of audit logging by storing messages in memory"""
101	
102	    def __init__(self) -> None:
103	        super().__init__()
104	        self.memory_log: list[tuple[tuple[str, datetime], str]] = []
105

108	        if timestamp is None:
109	            timestamp = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
110	        else:
111	            timestamp = timestamp.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
112	        self.memory_log.append(((id, timestamp), message))
113

123	
124	
125	class DynamoDBAuditLogger(AuditLogger):
126	    """DynamoDB backed audit logging"""
127	
128	    def __init__(self, table_name: str, dynamodb: DynamoDBClient) -> None:
129	        super().__init__()
130	        self.table_name: str = table_name
131	        self.dynamodb: DynamoDBClient = dynamodb
132	
133	    @override
134	    def log(self, id: str, message: str, timestamp: datetime | None = None) -> None:
135	        if timestamp is None:
136	            timestamp = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
137	        else:
138	            timestamp = timestamp.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
139	        item = self._create_item(id, message, timestamp)
140	        _ = self.dynamodb.put_item(
141	            TableName=self.table_name,
142	            Item=item,
143	            ReturnValues="NONE",
144	        )
145	        logger.info("Audit Message sent: %s", message)
146	
147	    @override
148	    def get_logs(self, id: str) -> list[AuditEntry]:
149	        paginator = self.dynamodb.get_paginator("query")
150	        pages = paginator.paginate(
151	            TableName=self.table_name,
152	            KeyConditionExpression="#id = :id",
153	            ExpressionAttributeNames={"#id": AuditEntry.ID_FIELD},
154	            ExpressionAttributeValues={":id": {"S": id}},
155	            ScanIndexForward=True,
156	        )
157	        results: list[AuditEntry] = []
158	        for page in pages:
159	            if "Items" not in page:
160	                continue
161	            for item in page["Items"]:
162	                results.append(self._item_to_entry(item))
163	        return results
164	
165	    def delete_logs(self, id: str) -> None:
166	        """Delete all audit log entries for the given erasure id from DynamoDB."""
167	        paginator = self.dynamodb.get_paginator("query")
168	        pages = paginator.paginate(
169	            TableName=self.table_name,
170	            KeyConditionExpression="#id = :id",
171	            ExpressionAttributeNames={
172	                "#id": AuditEntry.ID_FIELD,
173	                "#ts": AuditEntry.TIMESTAMP_FIELD,
174	            },
175	            ExpressionAttributeValues={":id": {"S": id}},
176	            ProjectionExpression="#id, #ts",
177	        )
178	        keys: list[dict[str, AttributeValueTypeDef]] = []
179	        for page in pages:
180	            for item in page.get("Items", []):
181	                keys.append(
182	                    {
183	                        AuditEntry.ID_FIELD: item[AuditEntry.ID_FIELD],
184	                        AuditEntry.TIMESTAMP_FIELD: item[AuditEntry.TIMESTAMP_FIELD],
185	                    }
186	                )
187	        for i in range(0, len(keys), DYNAMO_BATCH_WRITE_MAX_ITEMS):
188	            chunk = keys[i : i + DYNAMO_BATCH_WRITE_MAX_ITEMS]
189	            request_items: dict[str, list[WriteRequestTypeDef]] = {
190	                self.table_name: [{"DeleteRequest": {"Key": key}} for key in chunk]
191	            }
192	            for attempt in range(DYNAMO_BATCH_WRITE_MAX_ATTEMPTS):
193	                response = self.dynamodb.batch_write_item(RequestItems=request_items)
194	                unprocessed = response.get("UnprocessedItems") or {}
195	                if not unprocessed:
196	                    break
197	                # Cast: AWS returns UnprocessedItems in the same shape accepted by RequestItems
198	                request_items = cast(
199	                    "dict[str, list[WriteRequestTypeDef]]", unprocessed
200	                )
201	                time.sleep(min(0.1 * (1 << int(attempt)), 2))
202	            else:
203	                raise RuntimeError(
204	                    f"BatchWriteItem still has unprocessed deletes for {self.table_name} after {DYNAMO_BATCH_WRITE_MAX_ATTEMPTS} attempts"
205	                )
206	
207	    @classmethod
208	    def _create_item(cls, id: str, message: str, timestamp: datetime):
209	        timestamp_ms: int = int(timestamp.timestamp() * 1000)
210	        item = {
211	            AuditEntry.ID_FIELD: {"S": id},
212	            AuditEntry.TIMESTAMP_FIELD: {"N": str(timestamp_ms)},
213	            AuditEntry.MESSAGE_FIELD: {"S": message},
214	        }
215	        return item
216	
217	    @classmethod
218	    def _item_to_entry(cls, item: dict[str, AttributeValueTypeDef]) -> AuditEntry:
219	        id_value: str = item[AuditEntry.ID_FIELD][
220	            "S"
221	        ]  # pyright: ignore[reportTypedDictNotRequiredAccess]
222	        timestamp_ms: int = int(
223	            item[AuditEntry.TIMESTAMP_FIELD][
224	                "N"
225	            ]  # pyright: ignore[reportTypedDictNotRequiredAccess]
226	        )
227	        message: str = item[AuditEntry.MESSAGE_FIELD][
228	            "S"
229	        ]  # pyright: ignore[reportTypedDictNotRequiredAccess]
230	        timestamp: datetime = datetime.fromtimestamp(
231	            timestamp_ms / 1000, tz=timezone.utc
232	        )
233	        return AuditEntry(id=id_value, timestamp=timestamp, message=message)
234	
235	
236	def _s3_key_for(prefix: str, id: str) -> str:
237	    """Return the S3 key for a materialised audit log object."""
238	    if prefix:
239	        return f"{prefix}/{id}.jsonl.gz"
240	    return f"{id}.jsonl.gz"
241	
242	
243	class S3AuditLogReader:
244	    """Reads materialised audit logs from S3.
245	
246	    Each erasure request's complete audit log is stored as a single gzipped
247	    JSON Lines object at ``s3://{bucket}/{prefix}/{id}.jsonl.gz``.
248	    """
249	
250	    def __init__(self, bucket: str, prefix: str, s3_client: S3Client) -> None:
251	        self.bucket: str = bucket
252	        self.prefix: str = prefix.strip("/")
253	        self.s3: S3Client = s3_client
254	
255	    def _key_for(self, id: str) -> str:
256	        return _s3_key_for(self.prefix, id)
257	
258	    def get_logs(self, id: str) -> list[AuditEntry]:
259	        key = self._key_for(id)
260	        try:
261	            response = self.s3.get_object(Bucket=self.bucket, Key=key)
262	        except ClientError as e:
263	            error_code = e.response.get("Error", {}).get("Code", "")
264	            if error_code in ("NoSuchKey", "404", "NoSuchBucket"):
265	                return []
266	            raise
267	
268	        body_bytes: bytes = response["Body"].read()
269	        decompressed = gzip.decompress(body_bytes).decode("utf-8")
270	        entries: list[AuditEntry] = []
271	        for line in decompressed.splitlines():
272	            stripped = line.strip()
273	            if not stripped:
274	                continue
275	            entries.append(
276	                AuditEntry.from_json(json.loads(stripped))  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
277	            )
278	        return entries
279	
280	
281	class FallbackAuditLogReader:
282	    """Reads audit logs from a primary source, falling back to S3 when empty."""
283	
284	    def __init__(self, primary: AuditLogger, fallback: S3AuditLogReader) -> None:
285	        self.primary: AuditLogger = primary
286	        self.fallback: S3AuditLogReader = fallback
287	
288	    def get_logs(self, id: str) -> list[AuditEntry]:
289	        primary_results = self.primary.get_logs(id)
290	        if primary_results:
291	            return primary_results
292	        logger.info(
293	            "No audit logs found in primary store for id %s, falling back to S3", id
294	        )
295	        return self.fallback.get_logs(id)
296	
297	
298	class S3AuditLogWriter:
299	    """Writes a materialised audit log for a single erasure id to S3.
300	
301	    Produces the identical gzipped JSON Lines format that ``S3AuditLogReader``
302	    consumes, so ``FallbackAuditLogReader`` continues to work without changes.
303	    """
304	
305	    def __init__(self, bucket: str, prefix: str, s3_client: S3Client) -> None:
306	        self.bucket: str = bucket
307	        self.prefix: str = prefix.strip("/")
308	        self.s3: S3Client = s3_client
309	
310	    def write(self, id: str, entries: list[AuditEntry]) -> None:
311	        key = _s3_key_for(self.prefix, id)
312	        lines = "\n".join(json.dumps(entry.to_json()) for entry in entries)
313	        body = gzip.compress(lines.encode("utf-8"))
314	        _ = self.s3.put_object(Bucket=self.bucket, Key=key, Body=body)
315	        logger.info(
316	            "Archived %d audit log entries to s3://%s/%s",
317	            len(entries),
318	            self.bucket,
319	            key,
320	        )
321	
322	
323	class AuditArchiver:
324	    """Archives audit logs for an erasure request to S3 then removes them from DynamoDB.
325	
326	    Write order is intentional: S3 first, then DynamoDB delete. If the delete
327	    fails mid-way the DynamoDB copy still exists so ``FallbackAuditLogReader``
328	    will never return empty results. Retrying ``archive`` is safe — the S3
329	    object is overwritten and ``delete_logs`` tolerates already-deleted items.
330	    """
331	
332	    def __init__(self, primary: DynamoDBAuditLogger, writer: S3AuditLogWriter) -> None:
333	        self.primary: DynamoDBAuditLogger = primary
334	        self.writer: S3AuditLogWriter = writer
335	
336	    def archive(self, id: str) -> None:
337	        entries = self.primary.get_logs(id)
338	        if not entries:
339	            logger.info("No audit log entries to archive for id %s", id)
340	            return
341	        self.writer.write(id, entries)
342	        self.primary.delete_logs(id)
343	        logger.info("Audit log for id %s archived and removed from DynamoDB", id)
344	
345	
346	DYNAMO_BATCH_WRITE_MAX_ITEMS: Final[int] = 25  # AWS BatchWriteItem hard limit per call
347	DYNAMO_BATCH_WRITE_MAX_ATTEMPTS: Final[int] = 5
348	
349	DYNAMO_DB_AUDIT_LOGGING_TABLE: Literal["DYNAMO_DB_AUDIT_LOGGING_TABLE"] = (
350	    "DYNAMO_DB_AUDIT_LOGGING_TABLE"
351	)
352	"""Environment variable used for configuring the audit logging table"""
353	
354	AUDIT_LOG_S3_BUCKET: Literal["AUDIT_LOG_S3_BUCKET"] = "AUDIT_LOG_S3_BUCKET"
355	"""Environment variable used for the S3 bucket holding materialised audit logs"""
356	
357	AUDIT_LOG_S3_PREFIX: Literal["AUDIT_LOG_S3_PREFIX"] = "AUDIT_LOG_S3_PREFIX"
358	"""Environment variable used for the key prefix of materialised audit logs"""
359	
360	
361	def get_audit_logger() -> AuditLogger:
362	    """Get a concrete audit logger via environment variables"""
363	    table_name: str | None = os.getenv(DYNAMO_DB_AUDIT_LOGGING_TABLE)
364	    if table_name is not None:
365	        return get_dynamodb_audit_logger(table_name)
366	    else:
367	        logger.warning("No Audit Logging enabled")
368	        return NoOpAuditLogger()
369	
370	
371	def get_dynamodb_audit_logger(table_name: str) -> DynamoDBAuditLogger:
372	    dynamodb = boto3.client("dynamodb")
373	    return DynamoDBAuditLogger(table_name, dynamodb)
374	
375	
376	def get_s3_audit_log_reader(bucket: str, prefix: str) -> S3AuditLogReader:
377	    s3 = boto3.client("s3")
378	    return S3AuditLogReader(bucket=bucket, prefix=prefix, s3_client=s3)
379	
380	
381	def get_audit_log_reader() -> FallbackAuditLogReader:
382	    """Get an audit log reader configured from environment variables.
383	
384	    Uses the DynamoDB-backed audit logger as the primary source and an S3
385	    reader as the fallback. Both must be configured via environment.
386	    """
387	    primary: AuditLogger = get_audit_logger()
388	
389	    bucket: str | None = os.getenv(AUDIT_LOG_S3_BUCKET)
390	    if bucket is None or bucket == "":
391	        raise ValueError(f"{AUDIT_LOG_S3_BUCKET} was not set")
392	    prefix: str = os.getenv(AUDIT_LOG_S3_PREFIX, "")
393	
394	    fallback = get_s3_audit_log_reader(bucket=bucket, prefix=prefix)
395	    return FallbackAuditLogReader(primary=primary, fallback=fallback)
396	
397	
398	def get_s3_audit_log_writer(bucket: str, prefix: str) -> S3AuditLogWriter:
399	    s3 = boto3.client("s3")
400	    return S3AuditLogWriter(bucket=bucket, prefix=prefix, s3_client=s3)
401	
402	
403	def get_audit_log_archiver() -> AuditArchiver:
404	    """Get an AuditArchiver configured from environment variables.
405	
406	    Requires ``DYNAMO_DB_AUDIT_LOGGING_TABLE``, ``AUDIT_LOG_S3_BUCKET``, and
407	    optionally ``AUDIT_LOG_S3_PREFIX``.
408	    """
409	    table_name: str | None = os.getenv(DYNAMO_DB_AUDIT_LOGGING_TABLE)
410	    if table_name is None:
411	        raise ValueError(f"{DYNAMO_DB_AUDIT_LOGGING_TABLE} was not set")
412	
413	    bucket: str | None = os.getenv(AUDIT_LOG_S3_BUCKET)
414	    if bucket is None or bucket == "":
415	        raise ValueError(f"{AUDIT_LOG_S3_BUCKET} was not set")
416	    prefix: str = os.getenv(AUDIT_LOG_S3_PREFIX, "")
417	
418	    primary = get_dynamodb_audit_logger(table_name)
419	    writer = get_s3_audit_log_writer(bucket=bucket, prefix=prefix)
420	    return AuditArchiver(primary=primary, writer=writer)

# Plan: Address MR !62 review comment #1 (retry logic in `delete_logs`)

## Context

LyndonHB left an unresolved comment on `src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/audit_logging.py:201`:

> "I think this retry stuff would be best isolated a little more into its own code.
> I am also unsure at a glance what the `time.sleep` call does. Why are we doing a left bitwise shift?"

The current code in `DynamoDBAuditLogger.delete_logs` (lines 187–205) inlines three concerns into a single `for` loop:
1. chunking the key list into 25-item slices,
2. issuing `batch_write_item` and threading `UnprocessedItems` back into the next call,
3. exponential backoff via `time.sleep(min(0.1 * (1 << int(attempt)), 2))` and raising after `DYNAMO_BATCH_WRITE_MAX_ATTEMPTS`.

The `1 << attempt` is exponential backoff (1, 2, 4, 8, 16) but the bitshift is the source of the reviewer's confusion. The retry/backoff bookkeeping is also entangled with the chunking loop, making both harder to read and to test in isolation.

The intended outcome is: keep behaviour and the public API identical, satisfy the reviewer's two asks (isolate the retry, make the sleep self-explanatory), and preserve the existing tests in `tests/test_audit_logging.py` that monkeypatch `dns_de_rte_orchestrator.audit_logging.time.sleep`.

## Approach

Extract the retry/backoff logic into a private helper on `DynamoDBAuditLogger` and replace the bitshift with a named, documented backoff helper. No behaviour change.

### Changes in `src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/audit_logging.py`

1. **Add a module-level helper** `_backoff_seconds(attempt: int) -> float`:
   - Returns `min(BACKOFF_BASE_SECONDS * (2 ** attempt), BACKOFF_MAX_SECONDS)`.
   - One-line docstring naming this as capped exponential backoff.
   - Introduce two `Final` constants alongside the existing `DYNAMO_BATCH_WRITE_MAX_*`:
     - `BACKOFF_BASE_SECONDS: Final[float] = 0.1`
     - `BACKOFF_MAX_SECONDS: Final[float] = 2.0`
   - This replaces the inline `min(0.1 * (1 << int(attempt)), 2)` and removes the bitshift the reviewer flagged. `2 ** attempt` is the conventional, self-documenting form.

2. **Extract the retry loop** into a new private method
   `_batch_write_with_retry(self, request_items: dict[str, list[WriteRequestTypeDef]]) -> None`:
   - Owns the `for attempt in range(DYNAMO_BATCH_WRITE_MAX_ATTEMPTS)` loop, the `cast` of `UnprocessedItems`, the `time.sleep(_backoff_seconds(attempt))` call, and the `for/else` that raises `RuntimeError`.
   - Same `RuntimeError` message as today so the existing test
     `test_delete_logs_raises_after_max_attempts` (matches `"unprocessed deletes"`) keeps passing.
   - Crucially: still calls `time.sleep` from this module, so existing monkeypatches of
     `dns_de_rte_orchestrator.audit_logging.time.sleep` (used in
     `test_delete_logs_retries_unprocessed_items` and
     `test_delete_logs_raises_after_max_attempts`) continue to work unmodified.

3. **Slim `delete_logs`** to two responsibilities:
   - Build the keys list via the paginated query (unchanged).
   - For each chunk of `DYNAMO_BATCH_WRITE_MAX_ITEMS`, build `request_items` and delegate to
     `self._batch_write_with_retry(request_items)`.

### Files modified

- `src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/audit_logging.py` — sole code change.

### Files explicitly NOT modified

- `tests/test_audit_logging.py` — tests assert observable behaviour
  (number of `batch_write_item` calls, number of `sleep_calls`, the `RuntimeError` message)
  and patch `audit_logging.time.sleep`. All still hold after the refactor.
- `tests/test_dynamodb_audit_logger.py` — moto-backed integration tests, behaviour-only.

## Verification

1. `uv run pytest tests/test_audit_logging.py tests/test_dynamodb_audit_logger.py -q`
   — both `delete_logs` retry tests and the chunking test must still pass without edits.
2. `uv run pytest -q` — full suite green.
3. `uv run basedpyright src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/audit_logging.py` — no new diagnostics
   (the existing `cast` to `dict[str, list[WriteRequestTypeDef]]` moves into the helper unchanged).
4. Optional sanity: re-read the diff and confirm the body of `delete_logs` is now ~10 lines
   focused on chunking, with the retry concern fully owned by `_batch_write_with_retry`.

## Out of scope

The other 5 unresolved review comments (request_processor empty-case, archiver-in-`done`-branch,
`get_logs` sort-by-timestamp, typed `DuplicateRequestError`, README reorder) are tracked
separately and not addressed by this plan.

content.text:
The new backoff is **capped exponential backoff**, replacing the bitshift form. Mechanically identical sleep durations as today — only the expression changes.

**Old:** `time.sleep(min(0.1 * (1 << int(attempt)), 2))`
**New:** `time.sleep(_backoff_seconds(attempt))`, where:

```python
BACKOFF_BASE_SECONDS: Final[float] = 0.1
BACKOFF_MAX_SECONDS: Final[float] = 2.0

def _backoff_seconds(attempt: int) -> float:
    """Capped exponential backoff: 0.1s, 0.2s, 0.4s, 0.8s, 1.6s, … capped at 2s."""
    return min(BACKOFF_BASE_SECONDS * (2 ** attempt), BACKOFF_MAX_SECONDS)
```

**Sleep durations per attempt** (with `DYNAMO_BATCH_WRITE_MAX_ATTEMPTS = 5`):

| attempt | `0.1 * 2**attempt` | sleep (capped at 2s) |
|---------|---------------------|----------------------|
| 0       | 0.1                 | 0.1s                 |
| 1       | 0.2                 | 0.2s                 |
| 2       | 0.4                 | 0.4s                 |
| 3       | 0.8                 | 0.8s                 |
| 4       | 1.6                 | 1.6s                 |

So with 5 attempts the cap never actually fires — it only kicks in if `MAX_ATTEMPTS` is later raised (attempt 5 → 3.2s capped to 2s, etc.). It's defensive against future tuning.

**Why this satisfies the reviewer:**
- `2 ** attempt` is the textbook exponential-backoff form; `1 << attempt` did the same thing via integer bitshift but obscured intent.
- Named constants (`BACKOFF_BASE_SECONDS`, `BACKOFF_MAX_SECONDS`) replace the magic numbers `0.1` and `2`.
- The helper has a docstring stating "capped exponential backoff" so the intent is readable at the call site.

**What it is NOT:** no jitter, no full-jitter randomisation, no per-region budget. Just deterministic capped exponential — same policy as today, just legible. Adding jitter would be a behaviour change and isn't part of this comment.

Want me to add jitter as well, or keep the refactor strictly behaviour-preserving?

message.content: no jitter required. Implement the plan.

content.text:
Done. Here's a summary of what changed in `audit_logging.py`:

- **`delete_logs`** now just builds the key list and calls `self._batch_write_with_retry(request_items)` per chunk — the retry concern is gone from it.
- **`_batch_write_with_retry`** is a new private method owning the retry loop, `UnprocessedItems` threading, backoff sleep, and the `RuntimeError` after exhausting attempts.
- **`BACKOFF_BASE_SECONDS`** / **`BACKOFF_MAX_SECONDS`** replace the magic numbers `0.1` / `2`.
- **`_backoff_seconds(attempt)`** replaces `min(0.1 * (1 << int(attempt)), 2)` with the self-documenting `min(BACKOFF_BASE_SECONDS * (2.0**attempt), BACKOFF_MAX_SECONDS)`.

All 170 tests pass, no type errors or warnings.

- datalake-ci-permissions: Use when user asks to add or update any AWS resource deployment or GitLab CI IAM roles scoped to the datalake environment. Do not trigger for non-datalake environments (dev, prod, sandbox, services) unless explicitly requested.
- sam-template: Use whenever an AWS SAM template.yaml is being created or modified in the dns-de-automation lambda style, or any SAM template that integrates with serverless-access-manager. Trigger on direct requests ("add a new lambda", "update template.yaml", "add an SQS target to the access manager"), AND proactively whenever Claude is implementing code changes that end up adding or modifying a SAM template.yaml as a side-effect (for example: adding a new Lambda env var, wiring a new event source, adding an IAM permission, introducing a new SAM resource). If the implementation touches template.yaml at all, invoke this skill before writing the change.
- mr-description: Writes merge request message description when raising a GitLab Merge Request. Use when creating a merge request or when user asks to summarise changes for a merge request.
- commit-message: Generate concise git commit messages for Data Engineering changes. Use PROACTIVELY when the user asks to commit files, create a commit, or generate a git commit message.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the current diff and apply the fixes — equivalent to /code-review --fix.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

2	from abc import ABC
3	from datetime import datetime
4	from typing import Final

6	from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.as400 import AS400Notifier

11	    CustomerDetails,
12	)

18	from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.request_repository import RequestRepository
19	
20	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
21	
22	
23	class RequestProcessor:
24	    """Container for Erasure Request processing logic"""
25	
26	    PROCESS_STATES: tuple[ErasureState, ...] = (
27	        "new",
28	        "in-progress",
29	        "phase-1",
30	        "phase-2",
31	        "phase-3",
32	    )
33	    """The list of states (in order) that will have items processed"""
34	
35	    def __init__(
36	        self,
37	        repository: RequestRepository,
38	        audit: AuditLogger,
39	        as400: AS400Notifier,
40	        customer_api: CustomerAPIClient,
41	        dry_run: bool = False,
42	        archiver: AuditArchiver | None = None,
43	    ) -> None:
44	        self.repo: RequestRepository = repository
45	        self.audit: AuditLogger = audit
46	        self.as400: AS400Notifier = as400
47	        self.dry_run: Final[bool] = dry_run
48	        self.customer_api: CustomerAPIClient = customer_api
49	        self.archiver: AuditArchiver | None = archiver
50	        if dry_run:
51	            logger.warning("Request Processor is in dry-run mode")
52	
53	    def _check_timeout(self, timeout: datetime | None = None) -> bool:
54	        if timeout is None:
55	            return False
56	        now: datetime = datetime.now()
57	        return now >= timeout
58	
59	    def process(self, timeout: datetime | None = None):
60	        """
61	        Process the requests with an optional timeout to bail early before.
62	
63	        When working in a time limited environment like AWS Lambda, this
64	        timeout should be set a little before the whole Lambda will expire to
65	        account for interrupting the running logic.
66	
67	        The only time an error should be raised related to the timeout is if we
68	        timeout before the process has even begun as this indicates too short a
69	        timeout.
70	        """
71	
72	        if timeout is not None:
73	            logger.info("Process triggered with timeout of %s", timeout.isoformat())
74	        else:
75	            logger.info("Process triggered without timeout")
76	
77	        # This should do the following:
78	        # - Get all requests in a non-terminal state
79	        # - process each request appropriatelty
80	        # It should do this for each different non-terminal state in turn and
81	        # be checking for time remaining in-between each to make sure that we
82	        # have enough time left for this invocation, since this could be running
83	        # in an AWS Lambda.
84	        # We can also happily continue at the next function call since we will
85	        # be running often
86	
87	        if self._check_timeout(timeout):
88	            # Timed out before we began
89	            # This isn't right so raise an error
90	            raise ValueError("We timed out before we began")
91	
92	        # NOTE: We could improve the timeout logic by timning each previous
93	        # process. We'd then use this data to estimate if we'd timeout rather
94	        # than using a safety margin
95	
96	        # Collect some stats on the process
97	        failed_request_count: int = 0
98	        successful_request_count: int = 0
99	        processed_request_count: int = 0
100	        timed_out: bool = False
101	
102	        # NOTE: When we get all those requests in a given state, we may
103	        # well end up processing the same request through multiple states
104	        # if it has been transitioned to the next AND the storage layer has
105	        # consistently updated.
106	        for state in self.PROCESS_STATES:
107	            # Stop early if we've timed out
108	            # We can process this state in another iteration
109	            if self._check_timeout(timeout):
110	                logger.info('Timed out before checking state "%s"', state)
111	                timed_out = True
112	                break
113	
114	            all_in_state = self.repo.get_all_in_state(state)
115	            logger.info('Found %d entries for state "%s"', len(all_in_state), state)
116	
117	            for request in all_in_state:
118	                # Stop processing the items if we've timed out
119	                # We can pick back up in the next iteration
120	                if self._check_timeout(timeout):
121	                    logger.info(
122	                        'Timed out while checking state "%s" before request: %s',
123	                        state,
124	                        request,
125	                    )
126	                    timed_out = True
127	                    break
128	                # Process the single erasure request
129	                # We aren't using batch operations here so that we can be
130	                # idempotent as it relates to each individual request
131	                processed_request_count += 1
132	                try:
133	                    # We do this in a try block as we don't want the whole
134	                    # process to fail if individual requests do
135	                    self.process_request(request)
136	                    successful_request_count += 1
137	                except ErasureRequestException:
138	                    logger.exception("Problem processing request %s", request.id)
139	                    failed_request_count += 1
140	
141	        # After all the processing has been completed we need to quickly do
142	        # alerts as we may have timed out
143	        if processed_request_count > 0:
144	            logger.info(
145	                "Processed %d/%d requests successfully",
146	                successful_request_count,
147	                processed_request_count,
148	            )
149	            if failed_request_count >= processed_request_count:
150	                # Raise an exception if all processing failed.
151	                # This could be due to some misconfiguration so the calling
152	                # code should be alerted
153	                raise AllProcessingFailed(failed_request_count)
154	            if timed_out:
155	                # This logging message is partly redundent but if we often
156	                # time out it might be a bad sign
157	                logger.warning("Timed out while processing")
158	            if failed_request_count > 0:
159	                logger.error("%d requests failed to process", failed_request_count)
160	        else:
161	            logger.info("No requests processed")
162	            if timed_out:
163	                logger.warning("We timed out while checking states without finding any")
164	
165	def process_request(self, request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
166	        """Process a single request"""
167	        # This is where the meat of the business logic should be
168	        logger.info("Processing request: %s", request)
169	        self.audit.log(request.id, "Request was selected for processing")
170	
171	        # Defensively align the state to a standard lower case format
172	        request_state: str = request.state.strip().lower()
173	        if request_state not in VALID_STATES:
174	            raise InvalidRequestState(request)
175	
176	        cast_request_state: ErasureState = request_state
177	        match cast_request_state:
178	            case "new":
179	                self.process_new(request)
180	            case "in-progress":
181	                self.process_in_progress(request)
182	            case "phase-1":
183	                self.process_phase1(request)
184	            case "phase-2":
185	                self.process_phase2(request)
186	            case "phase-3":
187	                self.process_phase3(request)
188	            case "done":
189	                pass
190	            case _:
191	                raise NotImplementedError(
192	                    f'Request processing of state "{request_state}" not implemented'
193	                )
194	        if not self.dry_run and request.state == "done" and self.archiver is not None:
195	            try:
196	                self.archiver.archive(request.id)
197	            except Exception:
198	                logger.exception(
199	                    "Failed to archive audit log for request %s; will be retried on next invocation",
200	                    request.id,
201	                )
202	
203	    @classmethod
204	    def _validate_request(
205	        cls, current_state: ErasureState, request: ErasureRequest
206	    ) -> None:
207	        """
208	        Validate a request for the given current state.
209	
210	        This will raise an exception if the request is not valid.
211	        """
212	        if request.state != current_state:
213	            raise WrongStateForProcess(request, current_state)
214	
215	    def _transition_request(
216	        self,
217	        request: ErasureRequest,
218	        new_state: ErasureState,
219	        reason: str | None = None,
220	    ) -> None:
221	        """
222	        Helper function to transition a request to a new state.
223	
224	        You can provide an optional reason that will be used in audit logging.
225	        """
226	        logger.info("Transitioning request %s to %s", request.id, new_state)
227	        request.state = new_state
228	        if self.dry_run:
229	            logger.info("DryRun: Would update repository")
230	        else:
231	            self.repo.update(request)
232	        message = f"Request was transitioned to {new_state}"
233	        if reason:
234	            message = message + " due to: " + reason
235	        if not self.dry_run:
236	            self.audit.log(request.id, message)
237	
238	    def process_new(self, request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
239	        self._validate_request("new", request)
240	        logger.info("Processing new request %s", request.id)
241	
242	        # Check if there is an email attached to the request
243	        email: str | None = request.erasure_details.get("email")
244	
245	        if email is None or email.strip() == "":
246	            # Missing email address, this should always be present as the data
247	            # privacy team verify this in OneTrust before the request reaches
248	            # our system
249	
250	            logger.warning(
251	                "Request %s has no email so will be transitioned to need-more-detail",
252	                request.id,
253	            )
254	            # Update the request before raising an exception
255	            self._transition_request(request, "need-more-detail", "no email address")
256	            raise MissingEmailAddress(request)  # TODO: Should we raise?
257	
258	        # TODO: Update OneTrust in some way to tell it that we've started to
259	        # process a given request
260	
261	        self._transition_request(request, "in-progress")
262	
263	    def process_in_progress(self, request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
264	        self._validate_request("in-progress", request)
265	
266	        # TODO: In the original process this "in-progress" stage did not do a
267	        # great deal
268	
269	        # Enrich the request if possible or required
270	        self.enrich_request(request)
271	
272	        # TODO: Update OneTrust in some way to tell it that we've moved to the
273	        # next phase
274	
275	        # Move to the next state
276	        self._transition_request(request, "phase-1")
277	
278	    def process_phase1(self, request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
279	        self._validate_request("phase-1", request)
280	        logger.info("Running request %s through Phase-1", request.id)
281	        # This is the legacy system stage
282	        # For now all this should do is notify the AS400 team via both Slack
283	        # AND Microsoft Teams
284	        self.notify_as400_team(request)
285	
286	        # TODO: Update OneTrust that phase-1 was done
287	
288	        self._transition_request(request, "phase-2")
289	
290	    def process_phase2(self, request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
291	        # This is where the core of erasure logic happened in Tines
292	        self._validate_request("phase-2", request)
293	
294	        # TODO: Update OneTrust throughout each part of phase-2
295	
296	        # TODO: Subflow 1 from Tines:
297	        # TODO: Search for the customer in All Sites using the Customer API
298	        # TODO: Submit an erasure to the Customer Erasures API
299	        # If we found the sites for a customer we include those, otherwise it
300	        # is not included
301	
302	        # TODO: Subflow 2 from Tines:
303	        # TODO: Check the Customer Erasures API Status for the Erasure Request
304	        # If it's successful we then transition to the next subflow
305	
306	        # TODO: Subflow 3 from Tines:
307	        # TODO: Search and Delete Zendesk Tickets
308	
309	        # TODO: Subflow 4 from Tines:
310	        # TODO: Search and delete Zendesk users
311	
312	        # TODO: Subflow 5 from Tines:
313	        # TODO: Purge the deleted Zendesk tickets
314	
315	        # TODO: Subflow 6 from Tines:
316	        # TODO: Purge the deleted Zendesk users
317	
318	        # Finally after all this we transition to the next stage
319	
320	        raise NotImplementedError("phase-2 Request processing not implemented")
321	
322	    def process_phase3(self, request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
323	        self._validate_request("phase-3", request)
324	        # TODO: Phase-3 from Tines
325	        # This is just checking if the customer still appears in the customer
326	        # API or not
327	        # TODO: What should we do if the previous erasure states all failed?
328	
329	        # Finally transition to done
330	        raise NotImplementedError("phase-3 Request processing not implemented")
331	
332	    def enrich_request(self, request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
333	        """Enrich the erasure request if needed with details from other sources"""
334	        # Enrichment could include getting more details on the customer that
335	        # will be needed by downstream erasure processes
336	
337	        if "email" not in request.erasure_details:
338	            raise MissingEmailAddress(request)
339	
340	        # We will try and find details from all customer regions
341	        enrichment_data: list[CustomerDetails] = []
342	        enrichment_data.extend(
343	            self.customer_api.get_customer_details(
344	                email=request.erasure_details["email"]
345	            )
346	        )
347	        for site_id in ALL_SITE_IDS:
348	            enrichment_data.extend(
349	                self.customer_api.get_customer_details(
350	                    email=request.erasure_details["email"], site_id=site_id
351	                )
352	            )
353	
354	        found_site_ids: set[int] = set()
355	        fields_added: list[str] = []
356	        for entry in enrichment_data:
357	            # Enrich with the first found entries
358	            # If the customer has used the email in multiple regions with
359	            # different details this might enrich with weird values.
360	            # Essentially this is a best effort enrichment as email should be
361	            # enough for most systems to erase by, the extra details are just
362	            # useful for debugging purposes.
363	            if entry.first_name and "first-name" not in request.erasure_details:
364	                request.erasure_details["first-name"] = entry.first_name
365	                fields_added.append("first-name")
366	            if entry.last_name and "last-name" not in request.erasure_details:
367	                request.erasure_details["last-name"] = entry.last_name
368	                fields_added.append("last-name")
369	            if entry.loyalty_number and "rfl" not in request.erasure_details:
370	                request.erasure_details["rfl"] = entry.loyalty_number
371	                fields_added.append("rfl")
372	            if entry.mobile_number and "phone-number" not in request.erasure_details:
373	                request.erasure_details["phone-number"] = entry.mobile_number
374	                fields_added.append("phone-number")
375	            if entry.site_id:
376	                found_site_ids.add(entry.site_id)
377	
378	        audit_message: str | None = None
379	        if len(fields_added) > 0:
380	            fields_list = ",".join(fields_added)
381	            audit_message = (
382	                f"The following fields were added via enrichment: {fields_list}"
383	            )
384	        # Update site_ids
385	        current_site_ids = set(request.site_ids)
386	        if current_site_ids != found_site_ids:
387	            new_site_ids: list[int] = list(found_site_ids | current_site_ids)
388	            new_site_ids.sort()
389	            if new_site_ids != request.site_ids:
390	                request.site_ids = new_site_ids
391	                new_sites_list: str = ",".join(map(str, new_site_ids))
392	                sites_updated_msg = f"site_ids were updated to: {new_sites_list}"
393	                if audit_message is None:
394	                    audit_message = sites_updated_msg
395	                else:
396	                    audit_message += f"\n{sites_updated_msg}"
397	
398	        # Update audit log with details on enrichment
399	        if audit_message is not None:
400	            self.audit.log(request.id, audit_message)
401	
402	    def notify_as400_team(self, request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
403	        logger.info("Notifying AS400 of request %s", request.id)
404	        self.as400.notify(request)
405	
406	    def send_status_update(self, request: ErasureRequest, update: str) -> None:
407	        """Send a status update to OneTrust"""
408	        logger.info("Sending update to OneTrust for %s: %s", request.id, update)
409	        raise NotImplementedError("Sending status update to OneTrust not implemented")

412	class AllProcessingFailed(Exception):
413	
414	    def __init__(self, count: int) -> None:
415	        self.count: int = count
416	        super().__init__(f"Processing all {count} requests failed")
417	
418	
419	class ErasureRequestException(Exception, ABC):
420	    """Parent exception for Erasure Request process"""
421	
422	    def __init__(self, request: ErasureRequest, message: str) -> None:
423	        self.request: ErasureRequest = request
424	        super().__init__(message)
425	
426	
427	class InvalidRequestState(ErasureRequestException):
428	    """Exception raised when a request is not in a valid state"""
429	
430	    def __init__(self, request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
431	        self.state: str = request.state
432	        super().__init__(
433	            request=request,
434	            message=f"Request {request.id} is in an invalid state: {self.state}",
435	        )
436	
437	
438	class WrongStateForProcess(ErasureRequestException):
439	    """Exception raised when a request is in the wrong state for the given process."""
440	
441	    def __init__(self, request: ErasureRequest, expected: ErasureState) -> None:
442	        self.state: str = request.state
443	        super().__init__(
444	            request=request,
445	            message=f"Invalid state on request {request.id} for process: {self.state} != {expected}",
446	        )
447

import logging
from abc import ABC
from datetime import datetime
from typing import Final

from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.as400 import AS400Notifier
from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.audit_logging import AuditArchiver, AuditLogger
from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.customer_api_client import (
    ALL_SITE_IDS,
    CustomerAPIClient,
    CustomerDetails,
)
from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.erasure_request import (
    VALID_STATES,
    ErasureRequest,
    ErasureState,
)
from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.request_repository import RequestRepository

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


class RequestProcessor:
    """Container for Erasure Request processing logic"""

    PROCESS_STATES: tuple[ErasureState, ...] = (
        "new",
        "in-progress",
        "phase-1",
        "phase-2",
        "phase-3",
    )
    """The list of states (in order) that will have items processed"""

    def __init__(
        self,
        repository: RequestRepository,
        audit: AuditLogger,
        as400: AS400Notifier,
        customer_api: CustomerAPIClient,
        dry_run: bool = False,
        archiver: AuditArchiver | None = None,
    ) -> None:
        self.repo: RequestRepository = repository
        self.audit: AuditLogger = audit
        self.as400: AS400Notifier = as400
        self.dry_run: Final[bool] = dry_run
        self.customer_api: CustomerAPIClient = customer_api
        self.archiver: AuditArchiver | None = archiver
        if dry_run:
            logger.warning("Request Processor is in dry-run mode")

    def _check_timeout(self, timeout: datetime | None = None) -> bool:
        if timeout is None:
            return False
        now: datetime = datetime.now()
        return now >= timeout

    def process(self, timeout: datetime | None = None):
        """
        Process the requests with an optional timeout to bail early before.

        When working in a time limited environment like AWS Lambda, this
        timeout should be set a little before the whole Lambda will expire to
        account for interrupting the running logic.

        The only time an error should be raised related to the timeout is if we
        timeout before the process has even begun as this indicates too short a
        timeout.
        """

        if timeout is not None:
            logger.info("Process triggered with timeout of %s", timeout.isoformat())
        else:
            logger.info("Process triggered without timeout")

        # This should do the following:
        # - Get all requests in a non-terminal state
        # - process each request appropriatelty
        # It should do this for each different non-terminal state in turn and
        # be checking for time remaining in-between each to make sure that we
        # have enough time left for this invocation, since this could be running
        # in an AWS Lambda.
        # We can also happily continue at the next function call since we will
        # be running often

        if self._check_timeout(timeout):
            # Timed out before we began
            # This isn't right so raise an error
            raise ValueError("We timed out before we began")

        # NOTE: We could improve the timeout logic by timning each previous
        # process. We'd then use this data to estimate if we'd timeout rather
        # than using a safety margin

        # Collect some stats on the process
        failed_request_count: int = 0
        successful_request_count: int = 0
        processed_request_count: int = 0
        timed_out: bool = False

        # NOTE: When we get all those requests in a given state, we may
        # well end up processing the same request through multiple states
        # if it has been transitioned to the next AND the storage layer has
        # consistently updated.
        for state in self.PROCESS_STATES:
            # Stop early if we've timed out
            # We can process this state in another iteration
            if self._check_timeout(timeout):
                logger.info('Timed out before checking state "%s"', state)
                timed_out = True
                break

            all_in_state = self.repo.get_all_in_state(state)
            logger.info('Found %d entries for state "%s"', len(all_in_state), state)

            for request in all_in_state:
                # Stop processing the items if we've timed out
                # We can pick back up in the next iteration
                if self._check_timeout(timeout):
                    logger.info(
                        'Timed out while checking state "%s" before request: %s',
                        state,
                        request,
                    )
                    timed_out = True
                    break
                # Process the single erasure request
                # We aren't using batch operations here so that we can be
                # idempotent as it relates to each individual request
                processed_request_count += 1
                try:
                    # We do this in a try block as we don't want the whole
                    # process to fail if individual requests do
                    self.process_request(request)
                    successful_request_count += 1
                except ErasureRequestException:
                    logger.exception("Problem processing request %s", request.id)
                    failed_request_count += 1

        # After all the processing has been completed we need to quickly do
        # alerts as we may have timed out
        if processed_request_count > 0:
            logger.info(
                "Processed %d/%d requests successfully",
                successful_request_count,
                processed_request_count,
            )
            if failed_request_count >= processed_request_count:
                # Raise an exception if all processing failed.
                # This could be due to some misconfiguration so the calling
                # code should be alerted
                raise AllProcessingFailed(failed_request_count)
            if timed_out:
                # This logging message is partly redundent but if we often
                # time out it might be a bad sign
                logger.warning("Timed out while processing")
            if failed_request_count > 0:
                logger.error("%d requests failed to process", failed_request_count)
        else:
            logger.info("No requests processed")
            if timed_out:
                logger.warning("We timed out while checking states without finding any")

def process_request(self, request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
        """Process a single request"""
        # This is where the meat of the business logic should be
        logger.info("Processing request: %s", request)
        self.audit.log(request.id, "Request was selected for processing")

        # Defensively align the state to a standard lower case format
        request_state: str = request.state.strip().lower()
        if request_state not in VALID_STATES:
            raise InvalidRequestState(request)

        cast_request_state: ErasureState = request_state
        match cast_request_state:
            case "new":
                self.process_new(request)
            case "in-progress":
                self.process_in_progress(request)
            case "phase-1":
                self.process_phase1(request)
            case "phase-2":
                self.process_phase2(request)
            case "phase-3":
                self.process_phase3(request)
            case "done":
                pass
            case _:
                raise NotImplementedError(
                    f'Request processing of state "{request_state}" not implemented'
                )
        if not self.dry_run and request.state == "done" and self.archiver is not None:
            try:
                self.archiver.archive(request.id)
            except Exception:
                logger.exception(
                    "Failed to archive audit log for request %s; will be retried on next invocation",
                    request.id,
                )

    @classmethod
    def _validate_request(
        cls, current_state: ErasureState, request: ErasureRequest
    ) -> None:
        """
        Validate a request for the given current state.

        This will raise an exception if the request is not valid.
        """
        if request.state != current_state:
            raise WrongStateForProcess(request, current_state)

    def _transition_request(
        self,
        request: ErasureRequest,
        new_state: ErasureState,
        reason: str | None = None,
    ) -> None:
        """
        Helper function to transition a request to a new state.

        You can provide an optional reason that will be used in audit logging.
        """
        logger.info("Transitioning request %s to %s", request.id, new_state)
        request.state = new_state
        if self.dry_run:
            logger.info("DryRun: Would update repository")
        else:
            self.repo.update(request)
        message = f"Request was transitioned to {new_state}"
        if reason:
            message = message + " due to: " + reason
        if not self.dry_run:
            self.audit.log(request.id, message)

    def process_new(self, request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
        self._validate_request("new", request)
        logger.info("Processing new request %s", request.id)

        # Check if there is an email attached to the request
        email: str | None = request.erasure_details.get("email")

        if email is None or email.strip() == "":
            # Missing email address, this should always be present as the data
            # privacy team verify this in OneTrust before the request reaches
            # our system

            logger.warning(
                "Request %s has no email so will be transitioned to need-more-detail",
                request.id,
            )
            # Update the request before raising an exception
            self._transition_request(request, "need-more-detail", "no email address")
            raise MissingEmailAddress(request)  # TODO: Should we raise?

        # TODO: Update OneTrust in some way to tell it that we've started to
        # process a given request

        self._transition_request(request, "in-progress")

    def process_in_progress(self, request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
        self._validate_request("in-progress", request)

        # TODO: In the original process this "in-progress" stage did not do a
        # great deal

        # Enrich the request if possible or required
        self.enrich_request(request)

        # TODO: Update OneTrust in some way to tell it that we've moved to the
        # next phase

        # Move to the next state
        self._transition_request(request, "phase-1")

    def process_phase1(self, request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
        self._validate_request("phase-1", request)
        logger.info("Running request %s through Phase-1", request.id)
        # This is the legacy system stage
        # For now all this should do is notify the AS400 team via both Slack
        # AND Microsoft Teams
        self.notify_as400_team(request)

        # TODO: Update OneTrust that phase-1 was done

        self._transition_request(request, "phase-2")

    def process_phase2(self, request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
        # This is where the core of erasure logic happened in Tines
        self._validate_request("phase-2", request)

        # TODO: Update OneTrust throughout each part of phase-2

        # TODO: Subflow 1 from Tines:
        # TODO: Search for the customer in All Sites using the Customer API
        # TODO: Submit an erasure to the Customer Erasures API
        # If we found the sites for a customer we include those, otherwise it
        # is not included

        # TODO: Subflow 2 from Tines:
        # TODO: Check the Customer Erasures API Status for the Erasure Request
        # If it's successful we then transition to the next subflow

        # TODO: Subflow 3 from Tines:
        # TODO: Search and Delete Zendesk Tickets

        # TODO: Subflow 4 from Tines:
        # TODO: Search and delete Zendesk users

        # TODO: Subflow 5 from Tines:
        # TODO: Purge the deleted Zendesk tickets

        # TODO: Subflow 6 from Tines:
        # TODO: Purge the deleted Zendesk users

        # Finally after all this we transition to the next stage

        raise NotImplementedError("phase-2 Request processing not implemented")

    def process_phase3(self, request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
        self._validate_request("phase-3", request)
        # TODO: Phase-3 from Tines
        # This is just checking if the customer still appears in the customer
        # API or not
        # TODO: What should we do if the previous erasure states all failed?

        # Finally transition to done
        raise NotImplementedError("phase-3 Request processing not implemented")

    def enrich_request(self, request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
        """Enrich the erasure request if needed with details from other sources"""
        # Enrichment could include getting more details on the customer that
        # will be needed by downstream erasure processes

        if "email" not in request.erasure_details:
            raise MissingEmailAddress(request)

        # We will try and find details from all customer regions
        enrichment_data: list[CustomerDetails] = []
        enrichment_data.extend(
            self.customer_api.get_customer_details(
                email=request.erasure_details["email"]
            )
        )
        for site_id in ALL_SITE_IDS:
            enrichment_data.extend(
                self.customer_api.get_customer_details(
                    email=request.erasure_details["email"], site_id=site_id
                )
            )

        found_site_ids: set[int] = set()
        fields_added: list[str] = []
        for entry in enrichment_data:
            # Enrich with the first found entries
            # If the customer has used the email in multiple regions with
            # different details this might enrich with weird values.
            # Essentially this is a best effort enrichment as email should be
            # enough for most systems to erase by, the extra details are just
            # useful for debugging purposes.
            if entry.first_name and "first-name" not in request.erasure_details:
                request.erasure_details["first-name"] = entry.first_name
                fields_added.append("first-name")
            if entry.last_name and "last-name" not in request.erasure_details:
                request.erasure_details["last-name"] = entry.last_name
                fields_added.append("last-name")
            if entry.loyalty_number and "rfl" not in request.erasure_details:
                request.erasure_details["rfl"] = entry.loyalty_number
                fields_added.append("rfl")
            if entry.mobile_number and "phone-number" not in request.erasure_details:
                request.erasure_details["phone-number"] = entry.mobile_number
                fields_added.append("phone-number")
            if entry.site_id:
                found_site_ids.add(entry.site_id)

        audit_message: str | None = None
        if len(fields_added) > 0:
            fields_list = ",".join(fields_added)
            audit_message = (
                f"The following fields were added via enrichment: {fields_list}"
            )
        # Update site_ids
        current_site_ids = set(request.site_ids)
        if current_site_ids != found_site_ids:
            new_site_ids: list[int] = list(found_site_ids | current_site_ids)
            new_site_ids.sort()
            if new_site_ids != request.site_ids:
                request.site_ids = new_site_ids
                new_sites_list: str = ",".join(map(str, new_site_ids))
                sites_updated_msg = f"site_ids were updated to: {new_sites_list}"
                if audit_message is None:
                    audit_message = sites_updated_msg
                else:
                    audit_message += f"\n{sites_updated_msg}"

        # Update audit log with details on enrichment
        if audit_message is not None:
            self.audit.log(request.id, audit_message)

    def notify_as400_team(self, request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
        logger.info("Notifying AS400 of request %s", request.id)
        self.as400.notify(request)

    def send_status_update(self, request: ErasureRequest, update: str) -> None:
        """Send a status update to OneTrust"""
        logger.info("Sending update to OneTrust for %s: %s", request.id, update)
        raise NotImplementedError("Sending status update to OneTrust not implemented")


class AllProcessingFailed(Exception):

    def __init__(self, count: int) -> None:
        self.count: int = count
        super().__init__(f"Processing all {count} requests failed")


class ErasureRequestException(Exception, ABC):
    """Parent exception for Erasure Request process"""

    def __init__(self, request: ErasureRequest, message: str) -> None:
        self.request: ErasureRequest = request
        super().__init__(message)


class InvalidRequestState(ErasureRequestException):
    """Exception raised when a request is not in a valid state"""

    def __init__(self, request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
        self.state: str = request.state
        super().__init__(
            request=request,
            message=f"Request {request.id} is in an invalid state: {self.state}",
        )


class WrongStateForProcess(ErasureRequestException):
    """Exception raised when a request is in the wrong state for the given process."""

    def __init__(self, request: ErasureRequest, expected: ErasureState) -> None:
        self.state: str = request.state
        super().__init__(
            request=request,
            message=f"Invalid state on request {request.id} for process: {self.state} != {expected}",
        )


class MissingEmailAddress(ErasureRequestException):
    """Exception raised if the request is missing an email address"""

    def __init__(self, request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
        super().__init__(
            request=request, message=f"Missing email address on request {request.id}"
        )

from dataclasses import dataclass, fields
from datetime import datetime
from typing import Literal, override

type ErasureState = Literal[
    "new", "in-progress", "phase-1", "phase-2", "phase-3", "done", "need-more-detail"
]
"""Valid erasure states type"""

type ErasureDetailKey = Literal[
    "first-name", "last-name", "email", "postcode", "phone-number", "rfl", "last-order"
]
"""Expected erasure details keys"""

VALID_DETAIL_KEYS: frozenset[ErasureDetailKey] = frozenset(
    (
        "first-name",
        "last-name",
        "email",
        "postcode",
        "phone-number",
        "rfl",
        "last-order",
    )
)
"""Valid keys for erasure_details"""

VALID_STATES: tuple[ErasureState, ...] = (
    "new",
    "in-progress",
    "phase-1",
    "phase-2",
    "phase-3",
    "done",
    "need-more-detail",
)
"""Valid erasure states"""


@dataclass
class ErasureRequest:
    """
    An Erasure request as submitted by a source system.

    The normal source system is OneTrust.

content.text: Now let me update the plan file for this new task. Comment 2 is about the empty `case "done": pass` at `request_processor.py:189` — and it's tightly linked to comment 3 (which asks why archival isn't in `case "done"`). I'll show a plan that addresses comment 2 cleanly, which naturally subsumes comment 3.

input.content:
# Plan: Address MR !62 review comment #2 (empty `case "done": pass`)

## Context

LyndonHB left an unresolved comment on `src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/request_processor.py:189`:

> "What's the point in this empty case?"

…and a closely related one on line 201:

> "Should this not be called by the `case "done"` branch?"

The current `process_request` (lines 165–201) has this shape:

```python
match cast_request_state:
    case "new":         self.process_new(request)
    case "in-progress": self.process_in_progress(request)
    case "phase-1":     self.process_phase1(request)
    case "phase-2":     self.process_phase2(request)
    case "phase-3":     self.process_phase3(request)
    case "done":        pass                         # <-- comment 2
    case _:             raise NotImplementedError(...)
if not self.dry_run and request.state == "done" and self.archiver is not None:
    try:                                              # <-- comment 3
        self.archiver.archive(request.id)
    except Exception:
        logger.exception(
            "Failed to archive audit log for request %s; will be retried on next invocation",
            request.id,
        )
```

The empty case exists only because `"done"` is in `VALID_STATES` / `ErasureState` (see `erasure_request.py:5–7, 28–36`) — without it the match would fall into `case _` and raise `NotImplementedError`. The actual "done-state work" (archival) was placed *after* the match because today nothing inside `process_request` transitions to `"done"` mid-call (`process_phase3` is `raise NotImplementedError("phase-3 Request processing not implemented")`), so the only path that reaches archival is `case "done"` falling through to the post-match block. Splitting these into two separate places is what made the reviewer ask both questions.

The intended outcome: kill the empty case, give `case "done"` a real body that archives, and remove the duplicated post-match check.

## Approach

Move the archival logic *into* `case "done"` and delete the post-match `if … request.state == "done" …` block. This addresses comment 2 (the case is no longer empty — it does archival) and comment 3 (archival now lives in the `case "done"` branch the reviewer expected).

### Behavioural equivalence today

`process_request` is currently called only via `process()` (lines 106–135), which iterates `PROCESS_STATES = ("new", "in-progress", "phase-1", "phase-2", "phase-3")` — `"done"` is excluded. Inside `process_request`:

- For states `new` … `phase-2`, the matched handler calls `_transition_request` to the *next* phase, never to `"done"`.
- `process_phase3` is `raise NotImplementedError`, so it cannot transition to `"done"` either.
- The only way `request.state == "done"` is true at the post-match check today is if `process_request` was *entered* with state `"done"` — i.e. exactly the path that takes `case "done"`.

So moving the archival call from post-match into `case "done"` is a strict no-op for current behaviour. When `process_phase3` is later implemented and transitions to `"done"`, that implementation must call `self._archive_if_done(request)` (or recurse) — but that's a phase-3-implementation concern, out of scope here. We will leave a one-line `# NOTE:` comment in `process_phase3` to flag this so it isn't forgotten.

### Changes in `src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/request_processor.py`

1. **Extract the archival call** into a small private method on `RequestProcessor`:
   ```python
   def _archive_if_configured(self, request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
       if self.dry_run or self.archiver is None:
           return
       try:
           self.archiver.archive(request.id)
       except Exception:
           logger.exception(
               "Failed to archive audit log for request %s; will be retried on next invocation",
               request.id,
           )
   ```
   Keeping the same log message means existing tests (`tests/test_request_processor.py` archiver assertions — `cast(MagicMock, dynamo_mock.delete_logs).assert_called_once_with("req-1")` etc., found via `tests/test_audit_logging.py:914` flow) keep passing.

2. **Replace `case "done": pass`** with `case "done": self._archive_if_configured(request)`.

3. **Delete the post-match `if not self.dry_run and request.state == "done"` block** entirely.

4. **Add a one-line `# NOTE:`** in `process_phase3`'s body reminding the implementor to call `self._archive_if_configured(request)` (or re-dispatch via `self.process_request(request)`) after the transition to `"done"`. No behaviour change since the body is still `raise NotImplementedError`.

### Files modified

- `src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/request_processor.py` — sole code change.

### Files NOT modified

- `tests/test_request_processor.py` — existing tests assert the archiver is called when state is `"done"`, not called in dry-run, and that exceptions don't propagate. All three remain true after the move.
- No other call sites of `process_request` exist outside `RequestProcessor.process` itself.

## Verification

1. `uv run pytest tests/test_request_processor.py -q` — archiver tests must still pass without edits.
2. `uv run pytest -q` — full suite green.
3. `uv run basedpyright src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/request_processor.py` — no new diagnostics.
4. Visual sanity: `case "done"` now has a body; the post-match archival block is gone; `process_phase3` has a `# NOTE:` line about archiving on transition.

## Out of scope

The remaining 4 unresolved review comments (audit_logging retry refactor — already merged in plan-rev-1, `get_logs` sort-by-timestamp, typed `DuplicateRequestError`, README reorder) are tracked separately.
